content: Just as linguistic reflexion (as taught in high school ELA writing workshops) requires writers to step back from their draft to examine unstated assumptions and inconsistent logic against their core communicative goal, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by adopting the structured guided reflection framework used to teach student writers. This framework of targeted prompts asking 'what did I intend to say, what is unclear in my output, and what do I need to adjust' can reduce unproductive self-correction in current LLM reflexion agents.
